element.ToolTip: The unique name by which a range can be referred from other forms. When a range is created this field has an automatically generated value and can be changed by user. Currently a VLAN range can be referred by Emulated Router and IP ranges.
element.Caption: Name

element.ToolTip: The first ID to be used for the outer VLAN tag.
element.Caption: First Id

element.ToolTip: Frequency of outer VLAN ID increment. E.g., value of 10 means increment VLAN ID once for every 10 IP addresse.
element.Caption: Increment Step

element.ToolTip: Amount of increment per increment step for outer VLAN. E.g. increment step = 10 and increment = 2 means increment VLAN ID by 2 for every 10 Ips
element.Caption: Increment

element.ToolTip: Number of unique outer VLAN IDs to use.
element.Caption: Unique Count

element.ToolTip: The 802.1Q priority to be used for the outer VLAN tag.
element.Caption: Priority

element.ToolTip: The first ID to be used for the inner VLAN tag.
element.Caption: Inner First Id

element.ToolTip: Frequency of inner VLAN ID increment. E.g., value of 10 means increment VLAN ID once for every 10 IP addresse.
element.Caption: Inner Increment Step

element.ToolTip: Amount of increment per increment step for Inner VLAN. E.g. increment step = 10 and increment = 2 means increment VLAN ID by 2 for every 10 Ips
element.Caption: Inner Increment

element.ToolTip: Number of unique inner VLAN IDs to use.
element.Caption: Inner Unique Count

element.ToolTip: The 802.1Q priority to be used for the inner VLAN tag.
element.Caption: Inner Priority

element.ToolTip: Method used to increment VLAN IDs. May take the following values: 0 (Outer VLAN first), 1 (Inner VLAN first), 2 (Both).
element.Caption: Increment Mode

element.ToolTip: base value used when the plugin creates a mac addresses.
element.Caption: MAC

element.ToolTip: Incrementor used when the plugin creates a range of MACs.
element.Caption: Increment

element.ToolTip: Maximum Transmission Unit. The largest packet that a given network medium can carry. Ethernet, for example, has a fixed MTU of 1500 bytes, ATM has a fixed MTU of 48 bytes, and PPP has a negotiated MTU that is usually between 500 and 2000 bytes.
element.Caption: MTU

element.ToolTip: Activates the autonegotiation of the operation mode.
element.Name: cbAutoNeg
